mentions.note: Expensive software-as-a-service company pivoting rapidly; mixed fundamentals. Better than most enterprise software peers, but not a buy at current levels in today's cautious environment.
SaaS out of style alongside Salesforce, Adobe, Workday due to AI efficiency fears (fewer users = lower per-seat revenue).
Software rebound sharply higher today after recent weakness; benefiting from rebalancing flows and AI/data-center tailwinds.
Enterprise software group leader, up 5% today; part of rotation but sustainability questionable given prior reversal patterns.
Software stock in disfavored category. Market rotation away from software toward hardware/infrastructure.
Hammered on AI displacement fears dragging SaaS; analyst meeting Monday will reveal if customers shortening contracts; SaaS group fired up today.
Strong fundamentals (Rule of 50 compliance, huge sign-ups) masked by AI displacement fears; 44% YTD decline reflects multiple compression as pricing pressure emerges. Still expensive at 37x P/E.

AI rules-and-rails infrastructure; RPO up 23.5%, AI revenue guide raised to $1.5B. Stock down 30% from $240 peak. CEO McDermott confident, but market unclear on terminal value. Cramer neutral—cheap when discount deepens.
Heavy selling due to Anthropic disruption fears; expected solid quarter from Bill McDermott; good news should finally move stock after quarters of being ignored.
Expanded buyback and CEO buying at higher prices; still down 42% YTD; bounce may be oversold relief, not fundamental turn.
Enterprise software leader down nearly 8%; worst performer in IGV ETF; caught in severe software sector selloff.
Enterprise software incumbent facing AI disruption; high costs and lock-in moat weakening as AI firms offer simpler, more agile alternatives.
SaaS model intensely disliked on Wall Street; at 26x earnings is cheap for growth but sector turbulence expected to continue despite Cramer's respect for CEO McDermott.
Enterprise software bounce today signals overdue rebound; company furiously pivoting to combat AI competition; shouldn't have been priced like going out of business.
Big four enterprise software rally; soared 3.5% today on signs AI disruption fears reversing; earnings weakness not as severe as feared.
Part of software cohort rebound on sector rotation; Cramer sees beaten-down dog that benefited from program trade, not fundamental improvement.
Part of enterprise software rally; caught in program rotation but fundamentals warrant watching after rebound.
Enterprise software company vulnerable to Anthropic disruption; faces pressure from clients shortening contracts to test AI alternatives; avoid despite occasional bounces.
AI disruption narrative suggests clients may move to Claude-powered in-house development; earnings multiples at risk if contract terms shorten and pricing power erodes.
Had on show last week; down 50% from high; 19% earnings growth; 24x multiple down from 70x end-2024; announced 2B accelerated buyback; good entry point.
Enterprise software provider down 7% on AI substitution fears; multiple compression despite positive earnings and business momentum.
Down 24% after beat on every line and strong guidance; multiple crushed from 64x to 28x earnings; enterprise software group unbelievable selloff despite fundamentals.

Down 50% in 12 months despite excellent earnings/buyback. Multiple compressed 65x→42x→28x. Per-user model threatened by AI enabling fewer employees. Market voted no-confidence; wait for M to stabilize.

Beat on all metrics; $5B buyback with $2B accelerated repurchase; CEO bet personal comp on stock. Positioned as 'one of one' platform for agentic AI and business process reinvention; partnerships with OpenAI/Anthropic validate narrative; monthly active users +25% YoY; 1.3B seats remain untapped.
Biggest report of week in consequences; down 100+ points from high. CEO Bill McDermott must prove stock broken, not company. Glued to this one.
One-year low, down 100 bucks; enterprise software weakness across sector; chart pretty awful; Cramer won't call bottom, too hard to time.
Hit hard alongside other software names on AI replacement fears.
Software crushed by hardware/AI competition; 42x P/E still too high despite stock split; gave up points Friday, weak bounce today.
Cramer favorite with strong fundamentals; caught up in 2021 cloud software mania and repriced sharply lower in 2022; buy on weakness, not on rallies.
